_doc: Per-fact provenance for drawCardsReal.candidates.json. FACTS (nation, position, sourceStartYear, club membership) are sourced from Wikidata (CC0) and carry a resolvable ref. EDITORIAL fields (rating, fameRank, which <=3 clubs are printed, tag/displayCode/fullName, eraIndex, eraYear) are VerveQ's own and are marked as such — they are not claims about the player. NO DATE IS A PUBLISHED FACT: the card face carries no year at all (see sourceStartYearMeaning); sourceStartYear and eraYear live here in the dossier only. Built by scripts/buildDrawCardSet.ts, which is committed and reproduces this file exactly.

sourceStartYearMeaning.definition: The earliest senior-club membership START per the source's P54 statements, CLAMPED to the player's 16th year (E0.6): min over first-team starts of max(membershipStart, birthYear + 16). It is NOT a competitive-debut claim and is not published as one: the card face carries no year, and this value appears only here as membership-start provenance.
clampAndCuriosities: E0.6 — THE AGE-16 CLAMP replaced the age-16 DISCARD. A senior first-team membership
whose P580 predates the player's 16th year (an academy entry modelled on the
first-team QID, or a genuine child-prodigy debut) is anchored to born+16, not thrown
away. The discard let such a statement DELETE the earliest career point and promote a
later transfer to the anchor, sometimes across an era bucket (Agüero surfaced 2006 /
Atlético when his Independiente start is 2003). The clamp never deletes a career.
DEFINITIONAL RULING (E0.6 item 4, owner-signed): a sub-16 debut is a CURIOSITY, not
an anchor. The clamp IS the definition of the anchor — born+16, never the curiosity
year itself and never a later transfer. Radamel Falcao is the headline case: his
age-13 Gimnasia de la Plata 1999 statement clamps to born+16 = 2002, so his card
moves from bucket 3 (real-v3: 2005 River Plate) to bucket 2. This is accepted as the
pipeline's definition rather than patched per-player. The full set of pool players the
clamp re-buckets is in BUILD_NOTES; among selected cards: Falcao 3->2, David Luiz 3->2,
Mark van Bommel 2->1 (Agüero and Busquets are held in place by an override / ruling).
sourceStartYearMeaning.whyRenamed: E2.1's blind re-verify (drawCardsReal.verify2.json, committed) re-retrieved all 430 cards live and found the value systematically EARLIER than the competitive debut: ~25% of the cards for which a qualifying non-Wikidata source was actually retrieved were contradicted, spanning every era from Banks (1958) to Rashford (2016). Calling it debutYear asserted a debut the source cannot support. Renaming it to what it actually is — a membership start — makes every downstream claim true.
knownContaminationClasses: 1. SIGNING / SQUAD-REGISTRATION year — the year a player joined the club's books, not his first competitive minute (Owen 1995 signing vs 1997 debut; Banks 1953 part-time youth contract vs 1958 debut; Gattuso, Romero, Otamendi).

2. ACADEMY / YOUTH-INTAKE recorded on the FIRST-TEAM QID — one P54 statement starting at the academy age but typed as the senior club, so the age filter cannot split it (Busquets 2000 age-12 intake -> owner debut ruling; Szczesny 2006 academy vs 2009).
3. RESERVE / B-TEAM season counted as first-team (Moutinho 2003/04 Sporting B vs 2005 first-team; Benzema autumn-2004 CFA reserve side vs Jan-2005 first team).
4. SPLIT-SEASON label mis-parsed to its earlier year ('2005/06' -> 2005 for Cavani, whose Danubio debut was Apr 2006).
5. FRIENDLY / non-competitive match logged as a debut (David Silva's 2003 value traces to a Trofeo de La Magdalena friendly, not a competitive appearance).
sourceStartYearMeaning.note: These five are why sourceStartYear is provenance, not a truth claim about a debut. Where the contamination would put a card in the WRONG ERA, a signed cited override supplies eraYear instead (ownerCitedOverrides); where it does not cross a bucket it is accepted noise. printedSpells (per-club P54 spell dates) are likewise demoted to internal identity evidence: the published club fact is membership EXISTENCE only, never a date.

selector._doc: E1's selector was never committed and is unrecoverable (BUILD_NOTES §11). This is a fresh implementation of the documented rules; selection order, tier, club tags and display codes are DECLARED here, not recovered.
selector.fameBackbone: Wikidata sitelink count, descending; ties by QID ascending. EDITORIAL.
selector.clubPick: longest sourced first-team tenure (sum of P54 spells; open spell runs to 2026), ties by club QID

_doc: A game-mechanical partition of the set into four era buckets. NOT a truth claim: no
source asserts a player's era. eraIndex/eraLabel are VerveQ's own, like rating and
fameRank. The only input is eraYear (per-entry), which is the cited override where one
exists (ownerCitedOverrides) and the sourced sourceStartYear otherwise. Stated as a RULE,
not just ranges, so the partition can be recomputed from eraYear alone and can never
drift silently from the cards (asserted: drawCardSetEraContract).

rule.input: eraYear (per-entry; cited override where present, else sourced sourceStartYear)
rule.assign: lowest eraIndex whose peakYear range contains the card peakYear; ranges are contiguous and exhaustive

derivationNote: Why +5: a card belongs to the era a player was KNOWN in, not the year they first
appeared. A career start is typically followed by ~5 years before first-team prominence,
so bucketing on the start year alone files late-blooming players one era earlier than
fans place them. +5 is a flat editorial approximation of "time to prominence" —
deliberately uniform, since a per-player peak would be an unsourced judgement on all cards.
boundaryNote: The bucket0/bucket1 boundary (peakYear<=1980) is DECLARED, not transcribed, and is
LOAD-BEARING: bucket 0 sits exactly at the 40-card floor, so a single card crossing this
boundary forces a floor swap. E1.2 could declare it freely when no card had eraYear 1975;
that is no longer true. The choice is kept (it is the documented one, and it keeps the four
peakYear ranges contiguous with no gap) and is flagged for owner review. Every other
boundary is pinned by cards on both sides.

ownerPositionRulings.name: Neymar
ownerPositionRulings.note: A forward throughout his career (Santos, Barcelona, PSG). played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

ownerPositionRulings.name: Gareth Bale
ownerPositionRulings.note: Converted from left-back to a forward at Tottenham and played as a forward/winger at Real Madrid. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

_doc: Owner nation rulings for THE DRAW real card set (Ticket E0.3).
WHY THIS FILE EXISTS. E0.2 replaced E1.1's alphabetical nation pick (which made
Diego Costa 'Brazil' because B precedes S) with a sourced rule: a dual international
resolves to his MOST RECENT senior national team, read from the P580/P582 on the P54
statement itself. That rule is right far more often than it is wrong — it fixed Costa
to Spain, Zaha to Ivory Coast, Dumfries to Netherlands, Motta to Italy, Sammer and
Kirsten from GDR to Germany, Karpin and Mostovoi from CIS to Russia.
But recency is not identity, and on a handful of players it is confidently wrong in a
way no P54-only rule can see. The rule cannot know that Puskás's 4 Spain caps at 35
are a footnote to 84 caps for the Mighty Magyars — both spells are genuine, sourced
and dated, and Spain is simply later.
THE SIGNED PATTERN (identical to ownerPositionRulings.json). A ruling NEVER edits a
fact. playersSourced.json still emits the rule's answer, and cp-assert still proves it
reproduces from the rules. The override lives here and in the card/dossier, where it
carries provenance 'editorial-ruled' and reproduces the rule's output VERBATIM beside
it (ruleOutput + ruleCandidates), so a reader always sees what the rule said, what
Wikidata said, and that a human overruled it. Absence of a ruling is never read as
agreement: Di Stéfano was reviewed and deliberately NOT overridden (see below).
Rulings are applied to the POOL before selection re-runs — the ordering E0.2
discovered the hard way with positions. Nation does not enter the selection criteria
(fame + position quota + era floors), so unlike a position ruling it cannot change
which cards are in the set; it moves the nation histogram only.

reviewedNotOverridden.name: Alfredo Di Stéfano
reviewedNotOverridden.note: Reviewed and deliberately NOT overridden. The rule's answer is defensible on the merits rather than merely by recency: 31 caps for Spain against 6 for Argentina, and his Real Madrid era is what he is known for. Recorded here so that the absence of a ruling on this card is not mistaken for the case never having been looked at.

rulings.name: Ferenc Puskás
rulings.ruleBasis: P54: Hungary men's national football team 1945-1956; Spain men's national football team 1961-1962. Most recent senior national team wins; both spells are genuine playing careers and both overlap his club career, so the coaching-contamination guard does not fire.
rulings.note: The definitive Hungarian icon — 84 caps and 84 goals for the Mighty Magyars, the 1953 Wembley side, the 1954 World Cup final. His 4 Spain caps came at 35 after he had already been the best player in the world for a decade. Recency is a sourced tiebreak, not a claim about identity, and here it is wrong.

rulings.name: Edgar Davids
rulings.ruleBasis: P54: Netherlands national association football team 1994-2005; Suriname men's national football team 2009-2010. The Suriname span overlaps his club career (he was at Crystal Palace into 2010), so the coaching-contamination guard does not fire and recency picks Suriname.
rulings.note: 74 caps for the Netherlands across Euro 96, France 98, Euro 2000 and Euro 2004 — he is a Dutch international in every ordinary sense. The Suriname statement is unverified here and is a candidate F2 instance (inherited source falsehood): if it is false, no rule reading only P54 can detect it, and this override is the only instrument available. Flagged for the second-sourcing pass either way.

_doc: Owner debutYear rulings for THE DRAW real card set (Ticket E0.3).
WHY THIS FILE EXISTS. E0.2 ruled that debutYear is the FIRST-TEAM debut, which fixed
84 of 85 shifted debuts onto the commonly-cited year. It broke exactly one player, and
the reason is a fault in the SOURCE rather than in the rule: Wikidata records Sergio
Busquets's academy entry on the FIRST-TEAM QID — his FC Barcelona membership is a
single P54 statement, P580=2000, when he was 12, running to 2023. The age-16 filter
therefore discards that statement whole rather than clamping it, his reserve spells
(Barcelona C, Barcelona Atlètic) are excluded as reserve sides, and the only senior
first team left standing is Inter Miami — so the rule emits 2023.
E0.6 UPDATE. The pipeline now DOES read the clamped spell (age-16 clamp replaced the
discard), so the rule emits 2004, not 2023 — the '2004 (still wrong)' reading E0.2
measured is now the pipeline's own output. It is closer (a bucket off, not a career
off) but still not his debut, so the signed ruling stands. What changed materially is
the era impact: 2004 (peak 2009) is bucket 2 while the ruled 2008 (peak 2013) is bucket
3, so this ruling is now what holds the card in the 2010s bucket rather than a no-op.
THE SIGNED PATTERN (identical to ownerPositionRulings.json). A ruling NEVER edits a
fact: playersSourced.json still emits 2023 and cp-assert still proves it reproduces
from the rules. The override lives here and in the card/dossier, carrying provenance
'editorial-ruled' with the rule's output reproduced verbatim beside it.
Rulings are applied to the POOL before selection re-runs. debutYear DOES enter the
selection criteria via eraIndex (peakYear := eraYear + 5) and the >=40-per-bucket era
floor, so a debut ruling CAN move cards in and out of the set. Under E0.6 this one now
changes the card's bucket relative to the rule: ruled peakYear 2013 (bucket 3) vs rule
output peakYear 2009 (bucket 2). See eraImpact.

rulings.name: Sergio Busquets
rulings.ruleBasis: E0.6 CLAMP output. FC Barcelona is a single P54 statement, P580=2000 (age 12); under E0.6 it is CLAMPED to born+16 = 2004 rather than discarded, and 2004 is earlier than the only other surviving senior first team (Inter Miami CF 2023), so the rule now emits 2004 (was 2023 under E0.5's age-16 discard, when only Inter Miami survived). Still wrong — 2004 is his academy age, not a debut — but it is now off by a bucket, not by a career. FC Barcelona C and FC Barcelona Atlètic remain excluded as reserve sides; UFB Jàbac i Terrassa and UE Lleida are pre-16 youth stints.

independentRef.title: Busquets tested on 10 years at Barça
independentRef.supports: "Sergio Busquets made his first team debut on 13 September 2008. Under the orders of coach Pep Guardiola, the midfielder...shone in a 1-1 draw against Racing Santander."
independentRef.note: Fetched and read before recording — the page resolves, is the club's own publication, and names the exact debut date. A ref is only recorded here once it has been confirmed to resolve AND to support the ruled value.

rulings.independentRefStatus: RESOLVED (1 ref). E0.3 recorded this as OWED because no ref was supplied. E0.4 supplied two descriptors; the fcbarcelona.com piece was identified by its id (860614), fetched, verified to resolve and verified to state the debut date, and is recorded above. The second intended ref — a uefa.com appearances piece — carries no URL, id or slug, and NO URL was supplied when asked. It is therefore NOT recorded: a citation nobody can resolve is not a citation, and guessing a plausible uefa.com URL would be exactly the fabrication this pattern exists to prevent. The ruled value is independently sourced by the ref above; a second corroborating ref remains welcome but is not required for the value to stand.
rulings.note: His FC Barcelona first-team debut season. E1.1's BUILD_NOTES already recorded 2008 as the commonly-cited value while shipping 2006 ('Busquets 2006 (Barcelona C) vs 2008'), and E2's F4 lists him in the same reserve-set class as Pedro 2005-vs-2008 and Messi 2003-vs-2004. Ruled by the owner on 2026-07-17; the rule output was 2023 under E0.5 and is now 2004 under the E0.6 clamp — neither is a plausible debut for a player who won the Champions League in 2009.
rulings.eraImpact: E0.6 — NOW LOAD-BEARING (was 'none' under E0.5). The clamp moved the rule output from 2023 (peak 2028, bucket 3) to 2004 (peak 2009, bucket 2), so without this ruling the card would file into bucket 2 ('2000s'). The ruled value 2008 (peak 2013) keeps it in bucket 3 ('2010s-20s'), where it belongs. The card's eraIndex (3) is unchanged from real-v3; the ruling is what holds it there against the clamp.

_doc: Owner-signed DISPUTED STATEMENT records for THE DRAW real card set (Ticket E0.4).
WHY THIS FILE EXISTS — AND WHY IT IS NOT A RULE. E2's F2 finding is the one class
no rule in this pipeline can reach: a Wikidata statement that RESOLVES perfectly and
is simply FALSE. Every provenance check we have passes on it. E0.2's phantom rule
drops refs that do not resolve; E0.3's live sweep re-checked all 8436 memberships
against Wikidata and found 0 unresolvable refs. Neither sees a lie that is well
formed. E2 said so plainly: the only instrument that found any F2 instance was a
SECOND SOURCE, and all three it found were found by accident.
So this file is not a detector. It is a ledger of instances a human has established
by other means, signed, so that what we know does not live only in a session
transcript — the lesson BUILD_NOTES §11 paid for twice.
verdict 'notCredited' means: the statement is REAL, LIVE and preserved verbatim
below, but it is not credited as provenance for the fact it would otherwise support.
It is deliberately NOT dropped. Dropping it would be worse than keeping it: see the
Dybala record, where the disputed statement is the only thing carrying a value that
is independently CONFIRMED CORRECT.
EFFECT ON THE ARTIFACT. A disputed statement does not change a value — it changes
what we CLAIM about that value. The affected fact is forced to amber in the dossier
with this record attached, so a reader sees the value, the statement behind it, and
the reason the two do not add up. The canonical playersSourced.json is untouched:
a ruling never edits a fact, and cp-assert still proves the file reproduces from the
rules alone.
E0.5 EXTENSION — dropPrintedClub (the Benatia class). Where the disputed statement is
a CLUB the card would print but the player's first-team APPEARANCE there is disputed
(affectsFacts ['clubs']), the record carries dropPrintedClub = <clubQid>. The selector
suppresses that CHIP from the card face and fills the slot from the next club by tenure.
The membership still stays in the canonical AND in the dossier clubs (marked
disputedAppearance), so nothing is edited away — the card simply stops asserting a
first-team appearance a human has disputed.

records.name: Paulo Dybala

statement.verbatimNote: Preserved exactly as Wikidata carries it. Verified LIVE by E0.3's sweep and again on 2026-07-17: the ref resolves to a real, fully described club (multisports club / association football club). Nothing about the statement is malformed — that is the entire point of the F2 class.

records.dispute: Dybala never played for Club Sport Emelec. His real first senior club, Instituto de Córdoba, is absent from Wikidata's P54 for him entirely.
records.note: The committed sourceStartYear 2011 is CORRECT and stays. E2 confirmed it against independent Argentine press (senior debut for Instituto de Córdoba, 12 August 2011) — the value is right only because the phantom club and the real club happen to share a start year. Right value, invented provenance; correct by coincidence. This record exists because that coincidence is not a fact we should be silently trading on: the card claimed green off a statement that is false. It is now amber, with the reason attached. NOT dropped: E0.2 measured that dropping the Emelec statement moves his debut to Palermo 2012 — turning an independently confirmed CORRECT value into a wrong one. Emelec is not printed on the card (his three printed clubs are Juventus, Roma, Palermo by tenure), so the artifact has never asserted he played there.
records.e2Reference: E2 systemicFindings F2, severity UNMEASURABLE — 'Dybala (debut-setting statement places him at Club Sport Emelec, a club he never played for; his real first club is absent from Wikidata entirely; the committed value is right only because the phantom club and the real club share a start year)'. E2 amberList real_0104: 'Value 2011 CONFIRMED correct by independent Argentine press (Instituto de Cordoba, senior debut 12 Aug 2011) — but its provenance is a phantom Wikidata statement... Right value, invented provenance; correct only by coincidence of year.'

records.name: Mehdi Benatia

statement.verbatimNote: Preserved exactly as Wikidata carries it. The club is real and fully described (association football club); the statement resolves. What is disputed is the APPEARANCE, not the entity — the F2-adjacent case where the membership exists in the source but the first-team competitive appearance behind it does not.

records.dispute: ESPN's career history for Benatia omits Marseille ENTIRELY: its earliest season row is 2006/07 at Tours. He was in OM's system without a first-team competitive appearance. The printed Marseille chip (2005-2008) therefore asserted a first-team spell the appearance record does not support.
records.note: The Marseille CHIP is dropped from the card (the ticket's Benatia-class disposition). The membership STAYS in the canonical playersSourced.json as source data — a ruling never edits a fact — and stays in the dossier clubs marked disputedAppearance with this record. Only the chip is suppressed, so the card no longer asserts a first-team appearance a source disputes. The card's other clubs by tenure (Juventus, Bayern, Udinese) fill the three slots. Benatia's sourceStartYear is a SEPARATE within-bucket contradiction (2005 vs ESPN 2006, both era 3) recorded as accepted noise in ownerCitedOverrides — no era override.
records.e2Reference: E2.1 red real_0401: 'debutYear + printedSpells contradicted by non-Wikidata source... COMPOUND: ESPN's earliest season row is 2006/07 at Tours, and its career history omits Marseille ENTIRELY — so the card's printed Marseille spell (2005-2008) is itself questionable, not just the debut year. He was in OM's system without a first-team competitive appearance.'

_doc: Owner CITED ERA-YEAR overrides for THE DRAW real card set (Ticket E0.5).
WHAT THIS LAYER IS. sourceStartYear is the earliest senior-club membership START
per Wikidata's P54 statements (age >= 16, first teams). E2.1's blind re-verify
(drawCardsReal.verify2.json, committed) established that this is systematically
NOT the competitive debut: a signing / squad-registration / academy-intake /
reserve-season / friendly year sits where a debut was assumed on ~25% of the
cards for which a qualifying non-Wikidata source was actually retrieved.
The fact model was realigned rather than patched: no date is a published fact,
and sourceStartYear is published as membership-start provenance, not a debut. The
one place a year still DECIDES something the player sees is the ERA BUCKET
(eraYear -> peakYear := eraYear + 5 -> eraIndex). So this layer supplies eraYear.
RULE (Ticket E0.5 item 2). eraYear := citedValue where a record here exists, else
sourceStartYear. A record is minted ONLY for a verify2 RED whose contradiction
CROSSES an era bucket under the committed mapping — i.e. where the wrong year
would file the player in the wrong era. A within-bucket contradiction changes no
bucket and is recorded as ACCEPTED NOISE (see acceptedNoise below), NOT overridden:
minting a record that moves nothing would be a decision pretending to be one, the
same anti-pattern the debut/nation rulings guard against.
PROVENANCE. Every citedValue is taken from the verifier's OWN citation — publisher,
resolvable URL and verbatim quote are reproduced here, unaltered. This layer never
edits sourceStartYear: the sourced value stands beside the override in the dossier,
so a reader always sees what the source said and that a human overruled the ERA on
a named, quoted citation. The value the card renders (eraIndex/eraLabel) is the
only thing that changes.
KEY IS qid, NOT cardId. cardId is real_<fameRank> and fameRank is positional, so
it SHIFTS whenever the pool changes (E0.5 drops van der Sar and De Bruyne, moving
every lower rank up). Overrides are applied to the POOL before selection re-runs,
exactly like the position/nation/debut rulings, so they must key on the stable qid.
The cardId field below is the id AT THE TIME verify2 was written — an audit pointer
into that file's `red` array, expected to drift after the rebuild, never the key.
E0.6 UPDATE — THE AGE-16 CLAMP. E0.5 excluded a sub-16 statement by DISCARDING it;
E0.6 CLAMPS it to born+16 instead (max(membershipStart, born+16)), so an early
academy/curiosity P580 no longer deletes the earliest career point. This changes two
of the records above by construction: Aguero moved from an active override to
acceptedNoise (his 2003 debut clamps to 2004, same bucket as the cited 2003), and
Falcao is now re-bucketed by the clamp itself rather than debated as an override.
NOT HERE, ON PURPOSE:
- Falcao (real_0205 at real-v3, Q138172 — the E0.5 note misquoted the QID as
Q206567, which is absent from the pool; corrected here): verify2 flags a 1999
debut at age 13. Under E0.6's CLAMP his age-13 Gimnasia de la Plata 1999 statement
is anchored to born+16 = 2002 (not discarded), so sourceStartYear is 2002 and his
card moves from bucket 3 (real-v3: 2005 River Plate) to bucket 2 by the clamp. NO
cited override is minted: the definitional ruling is that a sub-16 debut is a
curiosity, not an anchor, and the clamp IS the definition of the anchor (born+16).
Recorded in DECISIONS.md and the dossier's sourceStartYearMeaning as a definitional
re-bucketing, and listed in the E0.6 clamp delta table (buildDrawCardSet BUILD_NOTES).
- Every within-bucket RED (Owen, Rashford, Cavani, Giroud, Ramsey, Gomez,
Szczesny, Bonucci, Gattuso, Romero, Otamendi, Banks, Sommer, Benatia-date): the
cited correction is real but changes no era bucket, so it is accepted noise. Note
Gordon Banks (real_0337): his 5-year error (1953 youth contract vs 1958 debut) is
the headline signing-vs-debut case, yet BOTH years sit in bucket 0 (peak <= 1980),
so no override is minted. The ticket's 'Banks class' names the contradiction
class, not a bucket crossing; Banks himself crosses nothing.

acceptedNoise.name: Sergio Aguero
acceptedNoise.quote: On 5 July 2003, Sergio Aguero (Argentina, b. 2 June 1988) made his Primera Division debut for Independiente aged 15 years 33 days.
acceptedNoise.note: E0.6 AMENDMENT — was an active override in E0.5 (bucketMove 3 -> 2). Under E0.5's age-16 DISCARD his Independiente 2003 statement (age 15) was thrown away, surfacing 2006 (Atletico Madrid arrival, bucket 3), so the cited 2003 debut crossed a bucket and the override was load-bearing. Under E0.6's age-16 CLAMP the same statement is anchored to born+16 = 2004 rather than discarded, so the derived anchor is now 2004 (bucket 2). Cited 2003 and derived 2004 both sit in bucket 2 (peak 2008/2009), so the correction crosses no bucket and moves nothing a player sees: accepted noise. citedValue 2003 is preserved here for audit; the card bucket is unchanged (2) by construction.

acceptedNoise.name: Ricardo Carvalho
acceptedNoise.note: 1996 disproven (debut at 20 => 1998/99) but no citation pins a replacement year; 1996 and 1998/99 both fall in bucket 2, so era is unaffected regardless.

_doc: Owner ERA RESTORES for THE DRAW real card set (Ticket E0.6).
WHAT THIS LAYER IS. The ingest's erased-earliest-career rule (E0.5) nulls
sourceStartYear when a player's true first career point is filed under a MALFORMED
non-club entity (a city, a commune, a business) whose start predates the earliest
surviving senior membership. That fail-closed is correct: no rule reading only P54
can recover the real start from a statement pointing at the wrong entity, so the
card fails out of the pool rather than ship a mis-bucketed year. But it drops two
icons whose era every fan knows. This signed layer re-admits exactly those two, on
an owner-cited eraYear, WITHOUT editing the canonical: playersSourced.json still
carries sourceStartYear null, cp-assert still proves it reproduces from the rules,
and the restore lives here and in the dossier as an editorial override — the same
discipline as a position or cited-era ruling. It is the ONLY thing that admits a
null-sourceStartYear card into the pool (see buildDrawCardSet.ts pool() and the
no-op guard: a restore whose card is NOT dropped is a STOP condition).
KEY IS qid. cardId is positional (real_<fameRank>) and shifts on every rebuild.
eraYear only. A restore supplies the ERA the card is bucketed into; it never claims
a sourced membership-start year (sourceStartYear stays null) and prints no date on
the card face. Club chips, position and nation come from the SURVIVING P54 — the
mis-filed early membership (van der Sar's Ajax under Q1492 the city) stays absent,
because it cannot be sourced, and no chip is invented to fill the gap.

records.name: Kevin De Bruyne
records.quote: De Bruyne made his first team debut for Genk in a 3-0 defeat at Charleroi on 9 May 2009.
records.erasedContext: sourceStartYear nulled by the erased-earliest-career rule: live Q357984 P54 carries a commune of Romania (Q12725066, Partestii de Jos, start 1995) and a pharmacy business (Q115689400, Farmacie Comunali Riunite, start 2000) — both malformed non-club targets whose starts predate his earliest surviving senior membership (K.R.C. Genk, P580=2008). The card was right by luck under earlier builds and 'right by luck' is not sourceable, so it failed the pool.
records.note: eraYear 2009 is his FIRST-TEAM debut, verified live at the Wikipedia URL above (fetched 2026-07-17, quote reproduced verbatim). His Genk MEMBERSHIP start is 2008 (P580) and both 2008 and 2009 map to bucket 3 (peak 2013/2014, '2010s-20s'), so the bucket is the same either way; 2009 is recorded because it is the sourced debut the citation actually supports. Club chips (Genk, Werder Bremen, Wolfsburg) and position (MID) / nation (Belgium) come from the surviving P54, unchanged.

records.name: Edwin van der Sar
records.publisher: Wikidata (Q482955, statement P54 -> Q1492), as recorded in the verify2 RED
records.quote: Live Q482955 P54 carries a 1990-1999 membership whose target is Q1492 = "Barcelona, city in Catalonia, Spain" - a CITY, not a club. The builder correctly refused the non-club entity, but the consequence is that the 1990-1999 span (his Ajax years) vanished ... and the earliest surviving club statement (Juventus, 1999) became the debut. 1999 is his Juventus transfer, not his debut.
records.erasedContext: sourceStartYear nulled by the erased-earliest-career rule: his 1990-1999 Ajax membership is the single malformed statement (filed under Q1492 = Barcelona the CITY), and 1990 predates the earliest surviving senior membership (Juventus, P580=1999). Q1492 is not a football club, so the chip is dropped too — Ajax appears nowhere on the card. This is honest: the source's own statement points at the wrong entity, and no rule can recover Ajax from it.
records.citationHonesty: NOT a resolvable external publisher+quote+url. The ticket asked for the citation to be 'lifted verbatim from his verify2.json RED record'; that record carries NO external corroboration. Its own words: 'A non-Wikidata confirmation of the Ajax 1990 debut was attempted but blocked (Britannica/Ajax/ManUtd/UEFA/ESPN/worldfootball all 403 or 404).' So eraYear 1990 is sourced to van der Sar's OWN Wikidata P54 Ajax statement (start 1990) — the same statement the erased-career rule refused for having the wrong TARGET, not the wrong START — and the quote above is the verify2 RED finding verbatim. A plausible external URL was deliberately NOT fabricated (the Busquets-ref discipline: a citation nobody can resolve is not a citation). 1990 -> peak 1995 -> bucket 1 ('1980s-90s'), one bucket earlier than the 1999-Juventus artifact that shipped in real-v3 (bucket 2).

position.note: A forward throughout his career (Santos, Barcelona, PSG). played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

citedOverride.name: Karim Benzema
citedOverride.note: First-team debut 15 Jan 2005 v Metz. Claimed 2004 traces to a reserve-team promotion / the 2004-05 season label; in autumn 2004 he played only Lyon's CFA fourth-division reserve side.

position.note: Converted from left-back to a forward at Tottenham and played as a forward/winger at Real Madrid. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

nation.note: The definitive Hungarian icon — 84 caps and 84 goals for the Mighty Magyars, the 1953 Wembley side, the 1954 World Cup final. His 4 Spain caps came at 35 after he had already been the best player in the world for a decade. Recency is a sourced tiebreak, not a claim about identity, and here it is wrong.
nation.ruleBasis: P54: Hungary men's national football team 1945-1956; Spain men's national football team 1961-1962. Most recent senior national team wins; both spells are genuine playing careers and both overlap his club career, so the coaching-contamination guard does not fire.

eraRestore.erasedContext: sourceStartYear nulled by the erased-earliest-career rule: live Q357984 P54 carries a commune of Romania (Q12725066, Partestii de Jos, start 1995) and a pharmacy business (Q115689400, Farmacie Comunali Riunite, start 2000) — both malformed non-club targets whose starts predate his earliest surviving senior membership (K.R.C. Genk, P580=2008). The card was right by luck under earlier builds and 'right by luck' is not sourceable, so it failed the pool.
eraRestore.note: eraYear 2009 is his FIRST-TEAM debut, verified live at the Wikipedia URL above (fetched 2026-07-17, quote reproduced verbatim). His Genk MEMBERSHIP start is 2008 (P580) and both 2008 and 2009 map to bucket 3 (peak 2013/2014, '2010s-20s'), so the bucket is the same either way; 2009 is recorded because it is the sourced debut the citation actually supports. Club chips (Genk, Werder Bremen, Wolfsburg) and position (MID) / nation (Belgium) come from the surviving P54, unchanged.

sourceStartYear.quote: De Bruyne made his first team debut for Genk in a 3-0 defeat at Charleroi on 9 May 2009.

position.note: A winger at Marseille and Bayern for over a decade. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.
A winger at Benfica, Real Madrid, PSG and Juventus. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

sourceStartYear.note: His FC Barcelona first-team debut season. E1.1's BUILD_NOTES already recorded 2008 as the commonly-cited value while shipping 2006 ('Busquets 2006 (Barcelona C) vs 2008'), and E2's F4 lists him in the same reserve-set class as Pedro 2005-vs-2008 and Messi 2003-vs-2004. Ruled by the owner on 2026-07-17; the rule output was 2023 under E0.5 and is now 2004 under the E0.6 clamp — neither is a plausible debut for a player who won the Champions League in 2009.
sourceStartYear.ruleBasis: E0.6 CLAMP output. FC Barcelona is a single P54 statement, P580=2000 (age 12); under E0.6 it is CLAMPED to born+16 = 2004 rather than discarded, and 2004 is earlier than the only other surviving senior first team (Inter Miami CF 2023), so the rule now emits 2004 (was 2023 under E0.5's age-16 discard, when only Inter Miami survived). Still wrong — 2004 is his academy age, not a debut — but it is now off by a bucket, not by a career. FC Barcelona C and FC Barcelona Atlètic remain excluded as reserve sides; UFB Jàbac i Terrassa and UE Lleida are pre-16 youth stints.

independentRef.title: Busquets tested on 10 years at Barça
independentRef.supports: "Sergio Busquets made his first team debut on 13 September 2008. Under the orders of coach Pep Guardiola, the midfielder...shone in a 1-1 draw against Racing Santander."
independentRef.note: Fetched and read before recording — the page resolves, is the club's own publication, and names the exact debut date. A ref is only recorded here once it has been confirmed to resolve AND to support the ruled value.

sourceStartYear.independentRefStatus: RESOLVED (1 ref). E0.3 recorded this as OWED because no ref was supplied. E0.4 supplied two descriptors; the fcbarcelona.com piece was identified by its id (860614), fetched, verified to resolve and verified to state the debut date, and is recorded above. The second intended ref — a uefa.com appearances piece — carries no URL, id or slug, and NO URL was supplied when asked. It is therefore NOT recorded: a citation nobody can resolve is not a citation, and guessing a plausible uefa.com URL would be exactly the fabrication this pattern exists to prevent. The ruled value is independently sourced by the ref above; a second corroborating ref remains welcome but is not required for the value to stand.

ownerRuling.note: Owner ruled Q483027 is Paolo Maldini, not his father Cesare (Q296350). The lone AC Milan anchor cannot separate them — this is a human decision on the corroborating evidence in ownerNote (b. 1968-06-26; exactly one club in P54; earliest senior P580 1985; DEF; Italy), not new club evidence.

position.note: A right winger at Barcelona, Real Madrid and Inter. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

disputedStatement.name: Paulo Dybala

statement.verbatimNote: Preserved exactly as Wikidata carries it. Verified LIVE by E0.3's sweep and again on 2026-07-17: the ref resolves to a real, fully described club (multisports club / association football club). Nothing about the statement is malformed — that is the entire point of the F2 class.

disputedStatement.dispute: Dybala never played for Club Sport Emelec. His real first senior club, Instituto de Córdoba, is absent from Wikidata's P54 for him entirely.
disputedStatement.note: The committed sourceStartYear 2011 is CORRECT and stays. E2 confirmed it against independent Argentine press (senior debut for Instituto de Córdoba, 12 August 2011) — the value is right only because the phantom club and the real club happen to share a start year. Right value, invented provenance; correct by coincidence. This record exists because that coincidence is not a fact we should be silently trading on: the card claimed green off a statement that is false. It is now amber, with the reason attached. NOT dropped: E0.2 measured that dropping the Emelec statement moves his debut to Palermo 2012 — turning an independently confirmed CORRECT value into a wrong one. Emelec is not printed on the card (his three printed clubs are Juventus, Roma, Palermo by tenure), so the artifact has never asserted he played there.
disputedStatement.e2Reference: E2 systemicFindings F2, severity UNMEASURABLE — 'Dybala (debut-setting statement places him at Club Sport Emelec, a club he never played for; his real first club is absent from Wikidata entirely; the committed value is right only because the phantom club and the real club share a start year)'. E2 amberList real_0104: 'Value 2011 CONFIRMED correct by independent Argentine press (Instituto de Cordoba, senior debut 12 Aug 2011) — but its provenance is a phantom Wikidata statement... Right value, invented provenance; correct only by coincidence of year.'

nation.note: 74 caps for the Netherlands across Euro 96, France 98, Euro 2000 and Euro 2004 — he is a Dutch international in every ordinary sense. The Suriname statement is unverified here and is a candidate F2 instance (inherited source falsehood): if it is false, no rule reading only P54 can detect it, and this override is the only instrument available. Flagged for the second-sourcing pass either way.
nation.ruleBasis: P54: Netherlands national association football team 1994-2005; Suriname men's national football team 2009-2010. The Suriname span overlaps his club career (he was at Crystal Palace into 2010), so the coaching-contamination guard does not fire and recency picks Suriname.

citedOverride.name: David Ospina
citedOverride.quote: Debuto en 2006 y por dos anos consecutivos el hincha verde durmio tranquilo.
citedOverride.note: Club's own site dates the debut to 2006, corroborated by Pulzo pinning the first league match to 5 Feb 2006 v Cucuta. 2004 unsupported by any source found.

disputedStatement.name: Mehdi Benatia

statement.verbatimNote: Preserved exactly as Wikidata carries it. The club is real and fully described (association football club); the statement resolves. What is disputed is the APPEARANCE, not the entity — the F2-adjacent case where the membership exists in the source but the first-team competitive appearance behind it does not.

disputedStatement.dispute: ESPN's career history for Benatia omits Marseille ENTIRELY: its earliest season row is 2006/07 at Tours. He was in OM's system without a first-team competitive appearance. The printed Marseille chip (2005-2008) therefore asserted a first-team spell the appearance record does not support.
disputedStatement.note: The Marseille CHIP is dropped from the card (the ticket's Benatia-class disposition). The membership STAYS in the canonical playersSourced.json as source data — a ruling never edits a fact — and stays in the dossier clubs marked disputedAppearance with this record. Only the chip is suppressed, so the card no longer asserts a first-team appearance a source disputes. The card's other clubs by tenure (Juventus, Bayern, Udinese) fill the three slots. Benatia's sourceStartYear is a SEPARATE within-bucket contradiction (2005 vs ESPN 2006, both era 3) recorded as accepted noise in ownerCitedOverrides — no era override.
disputedStatement.e2Reference: E2.1 red real_0401: 'debutYear + printedSpells contradicted by non-Wikidata source... COMPOUND: ESPN's earliest season row is 2006/07 at Tours, and its career history omits Marseille ENTIRELY — so the card's printed Marseille spell (2005-2008) is itself questionable, not just the debut year. He was in OM's system without a first-team competitive appearance.'

position.note: A winger/forward for Botafogo and Brazil's 1970 side. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.
